response.justification: Technically strong with extensive software development experience, a genuine open-source track record (GISCollective founder, multiple D libraries, FOSS talks), and solid Linux/Unix and architecture exposure, which aligns well with the role's open-source and digital-sovereignty focus. However, the role explicitly requires German at C1 minimum and the candidate lists only Basic German — a hard gap. Leadership and formal team-management evidence is also thin (mostly individual-contributor titles), and UML/modeling and Python are not demonstrated. Worth a screening call only if the German requirement is negotiable.
strengths: Extensive, broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and microservices
Strong open-source background and Linux/Unix orientation matching the company focus
Architecture and full-stack depth with TDD/CI/CD craftsmanship practices
concerns: German only at Basic level versus required C1 minimum
Limited evidence of formal team leadership or technical project management
UML/modeling and Python not demonstrated in the resume
